perturbation.original_text: 1. DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 1.1 In this Agreement unless the context otherwise requires the following words shall have the following meaning: "AFFILIATES" means any company, corporation, partnership, joint venture or other entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with XFN; "CONTENT" means real-time economic news including articles, reports, data, information and such materials that have or have been and/or will be published from time to time and that is or will be in the possession or control of CEIS from time to time, in respect of the subject matters as more particularly described in Schedule 1; EFFECTIVE DATE means 18 May 2000; "INTELLECTUAL means patents, trade marks, service marks, trade names, PROPERTY RIGHTS" design rights (whether registrable or not), any applications for the foregoing, copyright and other assignable intellectual property -1- rights (whether registrable or not) in any country, including but not limited to the format, layout, and the look and feel of any of the Content; "TERM" means the term as set out in Clause 7; and "TERRITORY" means the world excluding the People's Republic of China.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION 1.1 In this Agreement unless the context otherwise requires the following words shall have the following meaning: "AFFILIATES" means any company, corporation, partnership, joint venture or other entity that directly or indirectly controls, is controlled by or is under common control with XFN; "CONTENT" means general news including articles, reports, data, information and such materials that have or have been and/or will be published from time to time and that is or will be in the possession or control of CEIS from time to time, in respect of the subject matters as more particularly described in Schedule 1; EFFECTIVE DATE means 18 May 2000; "INTELLECTUAL means patents, trade marks, service marks, trade names, PROPERTY RIGHTS" design rights (whether registrable or not), any applications for the foregoing, copyright and other assignable intellectual property -1- rights (whether registrable or not) in any country, including but not limited to the format, layout, and the look and feel of any of the Content; "TERM" means the term as set out in Clause 7; and "TERRITORY" means the world excluding the People's Republic of China.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the definition of "CONTENT" from "real-time economic news" to "general news" creates an in-text contradiction. This is because the agreement specifies that CEIS is the owner and distributor of content relating to "financial and economic information", not general news. This change undermines the clarity of the agreement, blurring the lines between what type of news CEIS is licensing, potentially including content CEIS isn't entitled to license. The agreement now refers to both 'financial and economic information' and 'general news' as the content, making the scope of the license ambiguous and therefore creates uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS: (A) CEIS is the owner and distributor of certain content of Xinhua News Agency relating to financial and economic information; (B) CEIS wishes to appoint XFN as its licensee to distribute the content to users throughout the world in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'CONTENT' from 'real-time economic news' to 'general news'. This directly contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clause which states that CEIS is the owner and distributor of content relating to 'financial and economic information'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement now refers to both 'financial and economic information' and 'general news' as the content, making the scope of the license ambiguous and therefore creating uncertainty in enforcement, which falls under the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES 5.1 CEIS represents and warrants to XFN that during the Term of this Agreement: (a) CEIS is and shall remain entitled to grant to XFN the license to use the Content and other rights contained herein, free of all third-party liens, claims and encumbrances; (b) use of any Content by XFN in the manner contemplated by this Agreement does not and will not infringe any Intellectual Property Rights or other proprietary rights of any third party; (c) neither the Content nor any part thereof contains anything which is obscene, indecent, seditious, offensive, defamatory, threatening, liable to incite racial hatred, discriminatory, menacing or in breach of confidence; (d) the Content complies with and will comply with all applicable laws and regulations; (e) with respect to the provision of the Content, CEIS has acquired all requisite licenses, permissions and clearances for XFN to exercise the rights granted herein; (f) the Content is and will be reasonably accurate at the time of each delivery to XFN; (g) CEIS is a statutory body with legal person status validly existing under the laws of the People's Republic of China, being its jurisdiction of organization, and the execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement for and on its behalf has been duly and properly authorised by all required action, and Mr. Wang Zhongming, the Legal Representative of CEIS has been duly authorised to execute and deliver this Agreement for and on behalf of CEIS; (h) this Agreement is a valid and binding legal obligation enforceable against it in accordance with its terms; and (i) the execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement by it does not and will not: (i) require any authorization, consent, filing, registration or notice of or with any government agency in the People's Republic of China or Hong Kong; or (ii) result in any violation or breach of any agreement, obligation or order to which it is a party or to which it is subject.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES 5.1 CEIS represents and warrants to XFN that during the Term of this Agreement: (a) CEIS is and shall remain entitled to grant to XFN the license to use the Information and other rights contained herein, free of all third-party liens, claims and encumbrances; (b) use of any Information by XFN in the manner contemplated by this Agreement does not and will not infringe any Intellectual Property Rights or other proprietary rights of any third party; (c) neither the Information nor any part thereof contains anything which is obscene, indecent, seditious, offensive, defamatory, threatening, liable to incite racial hatred, discriminatory, menacing or in breach of confidence; (d) the Information complies with and will comply with all applicable laws and regulations; (e) with respect to the provision of the Information, CEIS has acquired all requisite licenses, permissions and clearances for XFN to exercise the rights granted herein; (f) the Information is and will be reasonably accurate at the time of each delivery to XFN; (g) CEIS is a statutory body with legal person status validly existing under the laws of the People's Republic of China, being its jurisdiction of organization, and the execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement for and on its behalf has been duly and properly authorised by all required action, and Mr. Wang Zhongming, the Legal Representative of CEIS has been duly authorised to execute and deliver this Agreement for and on behalf of CEIS; (h) this Agreement is a valid and binding legal obligation enforceable against it in accordance with its terms; and (i) the execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement by it does not and will not: (i) require any authorization, consent, filing, registration or notice of or with any government agency in the People's Republic of China or Hong Kong; or (ii) result in any violation or breach of any agreement, obligation or order to which it is a party or to which it is subject.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Content" to "Information" throughout Section 5, which contains CEIS's representations and warranties, without defining "Information," a significant contradiction is introduced. "Content" is defined in Section 1.1, and by using "Information" the warranties become ambiguous. It's unclear what CEIS is warranting—is it just the "Content" as defined, or some broader category of "Information"? This ambiguity weakens the protections afforded to XFN, because it is not known what legal protection they have. This could affect Section 6, the indemnity clause, because it isn't clear what CEIS will indemnify XFN against.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 CEIS shall fully indemnify XFN and hold XFN harmless from and against any and all costs, expenses, loss, damages, liabilities, claims and proceedings which may be incurred or suffered by or taken against XFN in relation to: (a) the exercise by XFN of the rights granted herein; and (b) any breach by CEIS of any provision of this Agreement or any act, Source: XINHUA SPORTS & ENTERTAINMENT LTD, F-1, 2/21/2007 default, omission or negligence of any nature on the part of CEIS and any of CEIS's officers, employees or agents and otherwise howsoever in connection with the rights hereby granted.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Content" to "Information" in Section 5, when "Content" is a defined term in Section 1.1 and "Information" is not, creates ambiguity. The indemnity clause in Section 6.1 refers to CEIS indemnifying XFN for breaches related to "the rights granted herein." If the rights are tied to "Content" as originally defined, but Section 5 now refers to the undefined term "Information," it's unclear what CEIS is warranting and, consequently, what they will indemnify XFN against. This directly impacts the scope and enforceability of the indemnity, creating a contradiction and weakening XFN's protections.

perturbation.original_text: 8. TERMINATION 8.1 XFN may terminate this Agreement by giving thirty (30) days written notice to the CEIS. 8.2 Either Party may terminate this Agreement: (a) if the other Party commits a material breach of this Agreement which is not capable of being remedied; (b) if the other Party commits a material breach of this Agreement which is capable of being remedied but not remedied within thirty (30) days upon receiving written notice from the non-breaching party requiring remedy; and (c) if the other Party becomes insolvent or bankrupt. 8.3 Upon termination of the Agreement: (a) CEIS shall terminate the transmission of the Content with immediate effect; and (b) in the event that this Agreement is terminated prior to the expiry of the Term, XFN shall recover any sums paid to CEIS in advance for the unexpired Term of this Agreement, together with interest from the date those sums were paid until the date of full refund.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. TERMINATION 8.1 XFN may terminate this Agreement by giving thirty (30) days written notice to the CEIS. 8.2 Either Party may terminate this Agreement: (a) if the other Party commits a substantial breach of this Agreement which is not capable of being remedied; (b) if the other Party commits a material breach of this Agreement which is capable of being remedied but not remedied within thirty (30) days upon receiving written notice from the non-breaching party requiring remedy; and (c) if the other Party becomes insolvent or bankrupt. 8.3 Upon termination of the Agreement: (a) CEIS shall terminate the transmission of the Content with immediate effect; and (b) in the event that this Agreement is terminated prior to the expiry of the Term, XFN shall recover any sums paid to CEIS in advance for the unexpired Term of this Agreement, together with interest from the date those sums were paid until the date of full refund.
perturbation.explanation: In section 8.2 (a), the phrase “material breach” has been replaced by “substantial breach”. While “material” and “substantial” may appear similar, they carry different legal connotations. The term "material breach" is a standard legal term that is often well-defined in case law and carries specific legal weight. By contrast, the term "substantial breach" is less well-defined. The inclusion of both “material” and “substantial” within the same section creates internal contradiction. It is unclear whether XFN can terminate the agreement if CEIS commits a substantial breach or a material breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 Either Party may terminate this Agreement: (a) if the other Party commits a substantial breach of this Agreement which is not capable of being remedied; (b) if the other Party commits a material breach of this Agreement which is capable of being remedied but not remedied within thirty (30) days upon receiving written notice from the non-breaching party requiring remedy; and (c) if the other Party becomes insolvent or bankrupt.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the changed text are nearly identical except for the replacement of "material breach" with "substantial breach" in section 8.2(a). The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text. According to the explanation, the introduction of both 'material breach' and 'substantial breach', where only 'material breach' was previously present, creates an in-text contradiction because they are different legal terms with potentially different implications for contract termination. The explanation highlights that 'material breach' is a well-established legal term, while 'substantial breach' may not be, leading to ambiguity and potentially conflicting obligations regarding contract termination. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the contract uses multiple terms without clear definitions, leading to conflicting obligations. Therefore, there is a clear contradiction introduced by the change.
